patches.find: The 1.67 nT/μGal coupling constant comes from dividing a disputed gravity reading by a magnetic measurement — both from the same unreplicated 1997 experiment. Later superconducting gravimeter measurements at multiple sites found zero gravity signal, which would make this ratio undefined.
patches.replace: The 1.67 nT/μGal coupling constant divides a magnetic anomaly measured at Boulder, Colorado (BOU magnetometer) by a gravity anomaly measured at Mohe, China — two different sites, different instruments, different events. The Mohe gravity result has not been independently replicated; later superconducting gravimeter measurements at multiple sites found zero gravity signal, which would make this ratio undefined.
patches.rationale: Fixes major factual error: TLDR incorrectly stated both numerator and denominator came from the same 1997 experiment. Numerator is BOU (Colorado), denominator is Mohe 1997 (China) — different sites. Curmudgeon WIN-012.c7 Hole 1 (major). Live since 2026-04-12.

patches.find: For the empirical refutations themselves, see WIN-013 (Membach 1999) and WIN-014 (Chinese SG network 2009).
patches.replace: For the empirical refutations themselves, see WIN-013 (Membach 1999) and WIN-014 (Chinese SG network 2009). For the dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart claiming to show κ via GRACE L1A residuals (BOU magnetic + L1A induction r = 0.992, AI = 1.152), see WIN-070.
patches.rationale: Adds missing WIN-070 cross-reference to Cross-references paragraph. WIN-070 is the dedicated rebuttal for the GRACE L1A impossibility argument relocated from pre-compression WIN-012. Curmudgeon WIN-012.c7 Hole 3 (moderate).

patches.find: Five of the eleven WINs added in late March 2026 (WIN-047, 048, 052, 054, 055) trace item-for-item to the modified-gravity / MOND community's canonical anomaly list — the Radial Acceleration Relation, El Gordo, the CMB Axis of Evil, Hubble tension, Cepheid tension. The remaining six (tidal constituents WIN-045/046/049/050/051 and dome-internal derivations WIN-044/053) fall under adjacent but distinct patterns: standard physics relabeled as dome predictions, and dome-internal failed derivations.
patches.replace: Five WINs from the late-March 2026 cohort (WIN-047, 048, 052, 054, 055) trace item-for-item to the modified-gravity / MOND community's canonical anomaly list — the Radial Acceleration Relation, El Gordo, the CMB Axis of Evil, Hubble tension, Cepheid tension. Seven other WINs in the same cohort (tidal constituents WIN-045/046/049/050/051 and dome-internal derivations WIN-044/053) fall under adjacent but distinct patterns: standard physics relabeled as dome predictions, and dome-internal failed derivations.
patches.rationale: P1: Fix TLDR arithmetic inconsistency (5+6=11 but explicit lists are 5+7=12). Decouple burst-count from MOND-grouping count; fix 'remaining six' → 'Seven other WINs in the same cohort'. Analyst proposal ISS-2154.

patches.find: and Cepheid distance-ladder tension (WIN-055). The remaining six belong to adjacent but distinct categories: tidal constituents M2/S2/K1/O1/N2 (WIN-045/046/049/050/051) are well-explained standard geophysical phenomena not addressed by MOND; a custom-derived firmament scaling function (WIN-044) and two-pole geomagnetic model (WIN-053) are dome-internal derivations whose refutations rest on their own terms.
patches.replace: and Cepheid distance-ladder tension (WIN-055). The remaining seven belong to adjacent but distinct categories: tidal constituents M2/S2/K1/O1/N2 (WIN-045/046/049/050/051) are well-explained standard geophysical phenomena not addressed by MOND; a custom-derived firmament scaling function (WIN-044) and two-pole geomagnetic model (WIN-053) are dome-internal derivations whose refutations rest on their own terms.
patches.rationale: P2: Fix 'remaining six' → 'remaining seven' to match actual enumeration (5 tidal + 044/053 = 7). Analyst proposal ISS-2154.

patches.find: The eleven WINs added between WIN-044 and WIN-054 in three commits on the morning of 2026-03-23 (04:57, 05:14, and 05:29 UTC — 32 minutes from first to last) are not a heterogeneous selection of dome-cosmology insights that happen to coincide with MOND-community talking points.
patches.replace: The WINs added between WIN-044 and WIN-055 in the late-March 2026 commit cluster (the body of which lands in three commits on the morning of 2026-03-23 at 04:57, 05:14, and 05:29 UTC — 32 minutes from first to last) are not a heterogeneous selection of dome-cosmology insights that happen to coincide with MOND-community talking points.
patches.rationale: P3: Reconcile body range with MOND-list inclusion of WIN-055. Decouples specific burst count from the range description to avoid the 'eleven WINs between WIN-044 and WIN-054' inconsistency with WIN-055 appearing in the MOND list. Analyst proposal ISS-2154.
